title: Cyrtochilum ballii
intro: Cyrtochilum ballii, also known as the Ball's Cyrtochilum, is a species of orchid native to Central America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyrtochilum ballii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cyrtochilum ballii is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to relieve respiratory issues and soothe coughs.
